parameters.content:
### Receive Inbound Webhook

Entry point: listens for POST calls on `/wf03487-document`. Use the TEST url while building, switch callers to the PRODUCTION url after publishing.

Next: Required Fields Present?

### Required Fields Present?

Two-way gate on `={{ $json.fileName }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Classify with AI · ②→ Abort — Malformed Input

### Abort — Malformed Input

Fails fast with a clear message; triggers the error workflow instead of processing bad data.

### Classify with AI

LLM classification into: Urgent, Routine, FYI (+fallback). Output port order matches this list.

Ports: ①→ Store as Urgent · ②→ Store as Routine, Capture Failure Context · ③→ Store as FYI · ④→ Alert Twilio — Other

⚠ failures exit via the error rail

### Groq Chat Model

Chat model powering the AI step (model: `llama-3.3-70b-versatile`). Swap providers here without rewiring.

Feeds **Classify with AI** as `languageModel`

🔑 Groq API key

### Store as Urgent

Inserts a row into the MySQL table using fields auto-mapped from the incoming item.

Ports: ①→ Reunite Branches · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 MySQL connection

### Store as Routine

Inserts a row into the MySQL table using fields auto-mapped from the incoming item.

Ports: ①→ Reunite Branches · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 MySQL connection

### Store as FYI

Inserts a row into the MySQL table using fields auto-mapped from the incoming item.

Ports: ①→ Reunite Branches · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 MySQL connection

### Alert Twilio — Other

Sends an SMS via Twilio — reserved for urgent alerts since SMS costs money per message, unlike chat notifications.

Ports: ①→ Reunite Branches · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Twilio SID + token

### Reunite Branches

Rejoins 4 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Load Previous Snapshot

### Load Previous Snapshot

Selects rows from the MySQL table (`returnAll`) — the read side of the store.

Ports: ①→ Compute Drift vs Snapshot · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 MySQL connection

### Compute Drift vs Snapshot

Custom JavaScript: Compare current metrics with the previous snapshot

Next: Stamp Completion Time

### Stamp Completion Time

Stamps `completedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Persist Run Metadata

### Persist Run Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Enrich with Reference Data

### Enrich with Reference Data

GET https://enrichment.example.com/v2/lookup — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Ports: ①→ Fold In Enrichment Fields · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Fold In Enrichment Fields

Shapes the item: writes `enrichedAt`, `enrichmentSource`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Offload to Post-Process Sub-workflow

### Offload to Post-Process Sub-workflow

Delegates to a reusable sub-workflow (single-responsibility building block) and waits for its result.

Ports: ①→ Record Sub-workflow Result · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail

### Record Sub-workflow Result

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Fingerprint for Idempotency

### Fingerprint for Idempotency

Custom JavaScript: Build a stable idempotency key per document (djb2 hash, no deps)

Next: Drop Already-Processed Items

### Drop Already-Processed Items

Cross-execution dedupe: remembers keys it has processed before and drops repeats — the idempotency guard.

Next: Serialize Run Output (CSV)

### Serialize Run Output (CSV)

Serializes items to a CSV binary file.

Next: Archive Row Snapshot

### Archive Row Snapshot

Inserts a row into the MySQL table using fields auto-mapped from the incoming item.

Ports: ①→ Tag Pipeline Metadata · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 MySQL connection

### Tag Pipeline Metadata

Shapes the item: writes `pipeline`, `version`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Guard Non-Empty fileName

### Guard Non-Empty fileName

Drops items that fail the condition; only qualifying items continue (no false branch).

Next: Cap Batch Volume

### Cap Batch Volume

Caps the stream at 25 items to protect downstream quotas.

Next: Align Field Names

### Align Field Names

Normalizes field names so every source feeds the same downstream schema.

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (MySQL)

### Append Audit Row (MySQL)

Inserts a row into the MySQL table using fields auto-mapped from the incoming item.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Twilio · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 MySQL connection

### Send Summary to Twilio

Sends an SMS via Twilio — reserved for urgent alerts since SMS costs money per message, unlike chat notifications.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Twilio SID + token

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#docs`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

Next: Park in Dead-Letter Queue

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Park in Dead-Letter Queue

Inserts a row into the MySQL table using fields auto-mapped from the incoming item.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 MySQL connection

# Summarize long emails with MySQL and Twilio without manual work

**Pattern:** AI Summarize & Route · **Level:** Architect · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Summarize long emails with MySQL and Twilio without manual work. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual contracts is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Receive Inbound Webhook → Required Fields Present? → Classify with AI → Store as Urgent → Reunite Branches → Load Previous Snapshot → Compute Drift vs Snapshot → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to ai summarize & route without manual effort
- Anyone automating contracts in legal
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Keeps your systems in sync
- Runs 24/7 in the background
- Responds in seconds

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Groq API key; MySQL connection; Slack OAuth2; Twilio SID + token.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

One Text Classifier node replaces what would otherwise be a chain of brittle IF/Switch keyword checks — the LLM handles the ambiguous cases a rules engine can't. Each category gets its own action node, so a failure in one branch (say, a bad Slack call) never blocks the others; Merge reunites them so the run still reports one clean completion.

## Practice exercises

About as deep as this pattern goes — extend it:

1. Add a 4th category with its own action branch.
2. Replace one action (e.g. a Slack ping) with a task-creation node and see how the wiring changes.
3. Feed the classifier deliberately ambiguous text and check which fallback category it lands in.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Failures are also parked in a dead-letter queue for replay. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🔐 Security

Never trust inbound payloads: the webhook path is unguessable. Add header auth or IP allow-listing for defense in depth. Secrets live in n8n credentials — never in node parameters.

## 🤖 AI cluster

Purple nodes form one AI unit: the root node orchestrates, sub-nodes plug in via typed ports (model / memory / tools / parser). Swap the chat model without rewiring anything else. Costs scale with tokens — keep prompts lean and cap iterations.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your contracts process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
